iran.headline: Trump announces 'very good and productive' US-Iran negotiations on March 24, 2026, postponing strikes on power plants; Israeli strikes continue in Tehran amid Strait of Hormuz tensions.
iran.summary: On March 24, 2026, President Trump stated the US held two days of productive talks with Iran to end the three-week war, postponing strikes on power plants and energy infrastructure while negotiations continue this week; Netanyahu affirmed Israel can reach a joint agreement protecting its interests.[3] Israeli forces conducted wide-scale airstrikes on Iranian infrastructure in Tehran on the same day, degrading military systems, as a David’s Sling malfunction allowed two Iranian missiles to hit southern Israel over the weekend.[3] Iranian-backed militias escalated attacks in Iraq, sending mixed signals; Strait of Hormuz remains tense with no imminent US operation to fully open it, despite weeks of reports.[3] This marks a potential de-escalation shift from pure kinetic action post-Operation Epic Fury and Khamenei's death, amid ongoing succession crisis with Assembly of Experts.
under_radar_flags: Iran parliament speaker eyed as potential US-backed leader
Iranian-backed militias escalation in Iraq with mixed signals
No imminent US operation to open Strait of Hormuz despite reports

israel.headline: Board of Peace expands with $1B permanent seat offers to multiple countries including Greece, Cyprus, and Indonesia on March 24, 2026, amid rejections and invitations
israel.summary: New invitations extended by the US to at least eight countries including Greece, Cyprus, and Indonesia for permanent seats on the Trump-created Board of Peace for Gaza reconstruction, requiring a $1B contribution, reported today.[1][3][9] Indonesia reaffirmed its rejection of the fee, limiting its role to peacekeeping and humanitarian aid, while sparking criticism of a 'pay-to-play' structure.[3][9] This development builds on the Board's ongoing Gaza mandate under EO 14375 with Israel as a key partner, amid elevated Israel intelligence posture post-Operation Epic Fury, but no new reports on Unit 8200 cyber operations, NSO Group, or Abraham Accords progress.
under_radar_flags: Board of Peace funding model criticized as pay-to-play, potentially accelerating Gulf-Abraham Accords capital bridge to Israeli tech via reconstruction
